annotations.label: Kurtosis

anchor.snippet: refers to the degree of tailedness in the probability distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `kurtosis` definition; only general moments via `ProbabilityTheory.centralMoment` exist.

annotations.label: Excess kurtosis

anchor.snippet: excess kurtosis is calculated as Pearson's kurtosis minus 3

annotations.note: No definition of excess kurtosis is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Platykurtic distribution (lead)

anchor.snippet: Negative excess kurtosis indicates a platykurtic distribution

annotations.note: Platykurtic terminology is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Leptokurtic distribution (lead)

anchor.snippet: positive excess kurtosis signifies a leptokurtic distribution

annotations.note: Leptokurtic terminology is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kurtosis as fourth standardized moment

anchor.snippet: The kurtosis is the fourth standardized moment

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `centralMoment X p μ` but has no standardized moment or kurtosis.

annotations.label: Lower bound on kurtosis

anchor.snippet: The kurtosis is bounded below by the squared skewness plus 1

annotations.note: Neither kurtosis nor skewness is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli realizes lower bound

anchor.snippet: The lower bound is realized by the Bernoulli distribution

annotations.note: No statement about kurtosis of Bernoulli exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Excess kurtosis of sum of independent variables

anchor.snippet: The excess kurtosis of Y is

annotations.note: Excess kurtosis is not defined, so the additivity formula is absent.

annotations.label: Kurtosis of sum of two variables

anchor.snippet: the kurtosis of the sum, X + Y , is

annotations.note: No kurtosis additivity result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moors' variable Z

anchor.snippet: where X is a random variable, μ is the mean and σ is the standard deviation

annotations.note: The Moors standardization construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Moors' identity for kurtosis

anchor.snippet: by the well-known identity

annotations.note: Moors' identity for kurtosis is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Entropy of a distribution

anchor.snippet: The entropy of a distribution is

annotations.note: Differential/probabilistic entropy of a distribution is not defined in Mathlib (only topological/dynamical entropies).

annotations.label: Normal distribution maximizes entropy

anchor.snippet: among all probability distributions

annotations.note: No maximum-entropy characterization of the Gaussian exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal entropy distribution for prescribed moments

anchor.snippet: then it is the maximal entropy distribution under the given constraints

annotations.note: No general maximum-entropy theorem with moment constraints in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kurtosis of maximum entropy distribution

anchor.snippet: then its kurtosis is

annotations.note: No formula for kurtosis of maximum-entropy distributions in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Excess kurtosis

anchor.snippet: The excess kurtosis is defined as kurtosis minus 3

annotations.note: Excess kurtosis is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mesokurtic distribution

anchor.snippet: Distributions with zero excess kurtosis are called mesokurtic

annotations.note: Mesokurtic terminology is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Normal distribution is mesokurtic

anchor.snippet: The most prominent example of a mesokurtic distribution is the normal distribution family

annotations.note: No kurtosis computation for `gaussianReal` exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Leptokurtic distribution

anchor.snippet: A distribution with positive excess kurtosis is called leptokurtic

annotations.note: Leptokurtic terminology is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Examples of leptokurtic distributions

anchor.snippet: Examples of leptokurtic distributions include the Student's t-distribution

annotations.note: Student's t-distribution is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Platykurtic distribution

anchor.snippet: A distribution with negative excess kurtosis is called platykurtic

annotations.note: Platykurtic terminology is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli with p=1/2 most platykurtic

anchor.snippet: The most platykurtic distribution of all is the Bernoulli distribution

annotations.note: No statement about minimal kurtosis of Bernoulli in Mathlib.

annotations.note: Pearson type VII family is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of moments in Pearson VII

anchor.snippet: The k -th moment exists provided

annotations.note: Pearson VII not formalized, so its moment-existence criterion is absent.

annotations.label: Limiting density in Pearson VII

annotations.note: No Pearson VII limiting-density result in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standard normal as limiting distribution

anchor.snippet: one obtains the standard normal density as the limiting distribution

annotations.note: The Gaussian-as-limit-of-Pearson-VII statement is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Comparison of well-known distributions

anchor.snippet: Several well-known, unimodal, and symmetric distributions from different parametric families are compared here

annotations.note: No comparative kurtosis table is formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: An alternative estimator of the population excess kurtosis, which is unbiased in random samples of a normal distribution

anchor.snippet: An upper bound for the sample kurtosis
